interactions.label: Grid Size
interactions.teaches: how changing the grid size affects the representation of the binomial filter

interactions.label: Value Height
interactions.teaches: how the height of the points corresponds to the filter's coefficients

interactions.label: Show Lines
interactions.teaches: the visual effect of connecting points with lines on understanding data structure

demo_steps.title: Understanding Grid Size
demo_steps.narration: Here, we're starting with a 3x3 grid which represents the binomial filter's core structure. Notice how the points are evenly distributed across the grid, symbolizing uniform filter application.

demo_steps.title: Visualizing Coefficient Heights
demo_steps.narration: Now, as the blue points rise, the heights illustrate the filter's coefficient values. Higher points show larger coefficients, reflecting their stronger influence in filtering.

demo_steps.title: Connecting the Points
demo_steps.narration: By toggling on the lines, you can see how each point is connected, forming a clear visual map of the filter coefficients. This connection highlights the coherence in the filtering process.

demo_steps.title: Exploring Larger Grid
demo_steps.narration: Let's expand the grid to a 5x5 size. Here, you can observe that the pattern repeats across a larger area, maintaining its characteristics, showing scalability of the filter.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: A three-quarters view showing both depth and spread of the grid to highlight the 3D structure clearly.
interactionPlan.notes: Consider a translucent effect for the grid lines for better visibility. Ensure lines are clear against the grid background.

gpt-4o.notes: Good geometric representation with strong interactivity; minor positioning and coloring differences.
